stimulus: Few moviegoers have heard James Horner's name. ______blank many have heard his work in the blockbuster film Titanic. As the film's composer, Horner created the haunting orchestral score and co-wrote the theme song that became a global hit.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. "However" logically signals that the claim in this sentence—that many moviegoers have heard Horner's work in Titanic—is true despite the previous information that few have heard his name.

Choice B is incorrect because "similarly" illogically signals that the two claims are similar, when in fact they contrast. Choice C is incorrect because "therefore" illogically signals consequence; the second sentence contrasts with rather than results from the first. Choice D is incorrect because "for example" illogically signals exemplification, when the second sentence offers a counterpoint.
